topic.name: Naval Warships of Major Powers
topic.description: This topic covers the design, development, and historical significance of various naval warships from major powers during the late 19th and early 20th centuries.

Document 1: The Russian battleship Sinop was built for the Imperial Russian Navy.
Sinop participated in the pursuit of the mutinous battleship Potemkin in June 1905.
Document 2: The Japanese aircraft carrier Unryū was the lead ship of her class of fleet aircraft carriers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy during World War II.
Unryū was torpedoed and sunk by the American submarine USS Redfish in the East China Sea.
Document 3: The H class was a series of battleship designs for Nazi Germany's Kriegsmarine.
Most of the designs had a top speed in excess of 30 knots.
Document 4: Mikasa is a pre-dreadnought battleship built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the late 1890s.
Mikasa served as the flagship of Admiral Tōgō Heihachirō throughout the Russo-Japanese War of 1904 – 1905.
Document 5: Libia was a protected cruiser built in Italy in the 1900s.
The ship had originally been laid down in 1907 for the Ottoman Navy.
Document 6: Admiral Scheer was a Deutschland-class heavy cruiser which served with the Kriegsmarine of Nazi Germany during World War II.
The vessel was named after Admiral Reinhard Scheer, German commander in the Battle of Jutland.
Document 7: The Asama-class cruisers were a pair of armored cruisers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy in the late 1890s.
They were part of the 'Six-Six Fleet' expansion program that began after the defeat of China.

topic.name: U.S. Highways and Routes
topic.description: The documents discuss various U.S. highways and state routes, detailing their histories, routes, and geographical significance.

Document 5: U.S. Highway 59 heads along the eastern portion of the state of Oklahoma.
US-59's route through Oklahoma takes it through the mountainous terrain of the eastern Oklahoma Ouachitas and Ozarks.
Document 7: Maryland Route 33 is a state highway in the U.S. state of Maryland.
MD 33 connects Easton, the county seat of Talbot County, with all communities on the peninsula.

topic.name: Themes in Music
topic.description: The documents explore various themes and narratives present in popular music across different genres and artists.

Document 1: 'Slow Fade' is a cautionary tale against making the wrong choices.
'Slow Fade' was written in the light of the well publicised falls from grace of several high profile church leaders.
Document 2: The album expresses other topics such as Jay-Z's thoughts on fatherhood, West's reflection on being deemed a social villain, and their legacy as performers.
Music writers interpreted the subject matter to concern the rappers' plight as African Americans struggling with financial success in America.
Document 3: The song received a positive reaction from contemporary critics in reviews of the track's parent album.
The song is played over footage of various performances during his Dangerous World Tour.
Document 4: The album is musically heavier and features darker lyrical content than the band's previous effort.
The album debuted at number four on the US Billboard 200, selling approximately 67,000 copies in its first week.
Document 5: The lyrics also urge people to unite to make the world a better place.
The track, thus, recalls previous Jackson songs that promote peace and environmentalism.
Document 6: The song describes the persona's desire to be rich in love, simultaneously comparing her lover with luxuries.
The track received mixed reviews from critics, who generally found it less impressive than the previous singles.
Document 7: The record's musical styles are diverse, incorporating the group's signature electropop and synthpop sound.
The album received favorable reviews from music critics, who noted that the other members had the opportunity to showcase their vocal abilities more.

topic.name: Professional Baseball Players
topic.description: The documents discuss the careers and lives of various professional baseball players in Major League Baseball (MLB).

Document 1: Pat Seerey was an American professional baseball player.
Seerey played in Major League Baseball (MLB) for seven seasons.
Document 2: Willard Hershberger was an American baseball catcher for the Cincinnati Reds of Major League Baseball (MLB).
Hershberger recorded a batting average of .316 in 160 career games.
Document 7: Allen Craig is an American professional baseball first baseman and outfielder in the Boston Red Sox organization.
He has played in Major League Baseball (MLB) for the St. Louis Cardinals and Boston Red Sox.

topic.name: Pacific and Atlantic Tropical Cyclones
topic.description: This topic covers the formation, impact, and historical significance of tropical cyclones in both the Pacific and Atlantic regions, including specific storms and their meteorological characteristics.

Document 1: Tropical Storm Rachel was the only tropical cyclone to make landfall during the 1990 Pacific hurricane season.
The storm produced heavy rainfall across northern Mexico and the Southwestern United States.
Document 2: The 1981 Pacific typhoon season has no official bounds; it ran year-round in 1981.
A total of 35 tropical depressions formed this year in the Western Pacific, of which 29 became tropical storms.
Document 3: Hurricane Iwa was at the time the costliest hurricane to affect the state of Hawaiʻi.
The hurricane devastated the islands of Niʻihau, Kauaʻi, and Oʻahu with wind gusts exceeding 100 mph.
Document 4: Tropical Storm Chris caused minor flooding in the Greater Antilles and the Eastern United States in August 1988.
Heavy rainfall occurred in Puerto Rico, which damaged 100 homes and caused three fatalities.
Document 5: Hurricane Alma was one of only four Atlantic tropical cyclones to reach hurricane status in the month of May.
After progressing northwestward and crossing western Cuba, Alma reorganized in the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 6: Hurricane Iris was the ninth named storm and fifth hurricane of the 1995 Atlantic hurricane season.
The hurricane accelerated to the north and absorbed a dissipating Tropical Depression Karen.
Document 7: The 1982 Florida subtropical storm was the only subtropical cyclone of the inactive 1982 Atlantic hurricane season.
The storm was the only system of 1982 to affect the eastern half of the United States.

topic.name: Fungi and Taxonomy
topic.description: The documents discuss various species of fungi, their characteristics, taxonomy, and ecological roles.

Document 1: Pholiota flammans is a basidiomycete agaric mushroom of the genus Pholiota.
Its edibility has not been clarified.
Document 2: Ramariopsis kunzei is an edible species of coral fungi in the Clavariaceae family.
It is commonly known as white coral because of the branched structure of the fruit bodies.
Document 4: Spongiforma thailandica is a species of fungus in the Boletaceae family, genus Spongiforma.
The stemless sponge-like species was found in Khao Yai National Park in central Thailand.
Document 6: Mycena nidificata is a species of fungus in the Mycenaceae family of the Agaricales.
The dark brown irregularly wrinkled cap measures up to 25 mm in diameter.

topic.name: Medieval Castles in England
topic.description: The documents discuss the history, architecture, and significance of various medieval castles in England, highlighting their construction, usage, and eventual decline or restoration.

Document 1: Stokesay Castle is a fortified manor house in Stokesay, Shropshire, England.
Restoration work was carried out in the 1830s and 1850s by William Craven.
Document 2: Beeston Castle is a former Royal castle in Beeston, Cheshire, England.
The castle was slighted in 1646, in accordance with Cromwell's destruction order.
Document 3: Longtown Castle is a ruined Norman motte-and-bailey fortification in Longtown, Herefordshire.
In the 21st century the castle is maintained by English Heritage and operated as a tourist attraction.
Document 4: Skipsea Castle is a Norman motte and bailey castle near the village of Skipsea, East Riding of Yorkshire, England.
Various archaeological investigations were carried out between 1987 and 2001.
Document 5: Dunstanburgh Castle is a 14th-century fortification on the coast of Northumberland.
The castle became the property of the Crown, before passing into the Duchy of Lancaster.
Document 6: Okehampton Castle is a medieval motte and bailey castle in Devon, England.
The de Courtenays prospered and the castle was further expanded to accommodate their growing household.
Document 7: Brownsea Castle was originally a Device Fort constructed by Henry VIII between 1545 and 1547.
The castle remained in use after the original invasion scare had passed.

topic.name: American Political Figures and Events
topic.description: The documents discuss notable American political figures, their careers, and significant events in U.S. history.

Document 1: Jane Fonda is an American actress, writer, political activist.
She is a two-time Academy Award winner.
Document 2: William Jason Fields was a politician from the U.S. state of Kentucky.
He represented Kentucky's Ninth District in the U.S. House of Representatives.
Document 4: Gary Johnson is an American businessman, politician and the Libertarian Party nominee for President.
He served as the 29th Governor of New Mexico.
Document 5: Thomas Cushing was an American lawyer, merchant, and statesman from Boston, Massachusetts.
He represented Massachusetts in the First and Second Continental Congresses.

topic.name: Olympic Participation and Athletes
topic.description: The documents discuss the participation of various nations and athletes in the Olympic and Paralympic Games, highlighting their achievements and challenges.

Document 1: Jamaica competed in the Winter Olympic Games for the first time at the 1988 Winter Olympics in Calgary, Canada.
They competed in one sport, Bobsledding, in both the two-man and four-man events and finished outside the medal places.
Document 2: Summer Mortimer is a Canadian paraswimmer who initially competed internationally for Canada, but switched to the Netherlands national paralympic team in 2014.
She has won seven world-championship finals for Canada and the Netherlands, and four medals for Canada at her first Paralympic Games in London.
Document 7: Bermuda sent a delegation to compete at the 2008 Summer Olympics in Beijing, China.
The games marked Bermuda's twenty-first Olympic appearance since its debut in 1936.

topic.name: Cultural and Historical Significance
topic.description: The documents explore various aspects of cultural and historical significance, ranging from sports achievements to notable films, battles, and music evolution.

Document 1: Ramone Moore is an American professional basketball player.
Moore led the Philadelphia Public League in scoring as a senior and earned Public League MVP honors.
Document 2: Forbidden Zone is a 1980 American musical fantasy comedy film directed and produced by Richard Elfman.
The film developed a cult following.
Document 3: The Battle of Kleidion took place on July 29, 1014 between the Byzantine Empire and the Bulgarian Empire.
The battle was a major defeat for the Bulgarians.
Document 4: Siward Barn was an 11th-century English thegn and landowner-warrior.
Siward's resistance continued until his capture on the Isle of Ely.
Document 5: 'Baby, Please Don't Go' is a blues song which has been called one of the most played pieces in blues history.
The song's roots have been traced back to nineteenth-century American songs.
Document 6: The River Witham is a river almost entirely in the county of Lincolnshire.
Archaeological and documentary evidence shows the importance of the Witham as a navigation from the Iron Age onwards.
Document 7: 'Lice' is the tenth episode of the ninth season of The Office.
The series depicts the everyday lives of office employees in the Scranton, Pennsylvania branch.

topic.name: Infrastructure and Architecture
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of infrastructure and architectural developments in different regions, highlighting historical influences and technological advancements.

Document 1: The architecture of Norway has evolved in response to changing economic conditions, technological advances, demographic fluctuations and cultural shifts.
distinctive traditions in Norwegian vernacular architecture
Document 2: Taiwan High Speed Rail is a high-speed rail line that runs approximately 345 km along the west coast of Taiwan.
the total cost of the project was US $ 18 billion.
Document 3: The Roa – Hønefoss Line is a 32-kilometer long railway line between Roa and Hønefoss in Norway.
The line was electrified in 1961.
Document 5: Skarnsund Bridge is a 1,010-metre long concrete cable-stayed bridge that crosses the Skarnsundet sound.
the bridge is the only road crossing of the Trondheimsfjord.

topic.name: Auto Racing Events
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various auto racing events, including Formula One and NASCAR races, detailing race outcomes, notable drivers, and key moments.

Document 1: The 2008 French Grand Prix was a Formula One motor race held on 22 June 2008 at the Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours, France.
The race was won by Felipe Massa for the Ferrari team after starting from second position.
Document 2: The 2011 Subway Fresh Fit 500 was a NASCAR Sprint Cup Series stock car race held on February 27, 2011 at Phoenix International Raceway.
It was won by Jeff Gordon for Hendrick Motorsports.
Document 3: The 2008 UAW-Dodge 400 was the third stock car race of the 2008 NASCAR Sprint Cup Series.
It was held on March 2, 2008, before a crowd of 153,000 in Las Vegas, Nevada.
Document 4: The 2010 Ford 400 was a NASCAR Sprint Cup Series stock car race that was held on November 21, 2010 at Homestead Miami Speedway.
The race was won by Carl Edwards for the Roush Fenway Racing team.
Document 5: The 2007 24 Hours of Le Mans was the 75th Grand Prix of Endurance, and took place at the Circuit de la Sarthe, Le Mans, France.
The race was attended by over 250,000 spectators.

topic.name: Historical Conflicts and Traditions
topic.description: The documents discuss various historical conflicts and cultural traditions across different regions and time periods.

Document 1: The Smolensk War (1632 – 1634) was a conflict fought between the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth and Russia.
Hostilities began in October 1632 when Russian forces tried to capture the city of Smolensk.
Document 2: The Raid on Unadilla and Onaquaga was a series of military operations by Continental Army forces and New York militia against the Iroquois towns.
The raid was executed in retaliation for a series of raids on frontier communities.
Document 5: Hoodening is a folk custom found in Kent, a county in south-eastern England.
It represents a regional variation of a 'hooded animal' tradition that appears in various forms throughout the British Isles.
Document 6: The Mari Lwyd is a wassailing folk custom found in South Wales.
It represents a regional variation of a 'hooded animal' tradition that appears in various forms throughout Britain.

topic.name: Culinary Practices and Trends
topic.description: This topic encompasses various cooking methods, food preparation techniques, and the evolution of menu items in the culinary world.

Document 1: Deep frying is a cooking method in which food is submerged in hot fat.
Deep frying is popular worldwide, with deep-fried foods accounting for a large portion of global caloric consumption.
Document 2: Burger King was the first major fast food chain to introduce a grilled chicken sandwich to the marketplace.
The TenderGrill sandwich was introduced as part of a series of sandwiches designed to expand Burger King 's menu with both more sophisticated, adult oriented fare.

topic.name: Comics and Graphic Novels
topic.description: The documents explore various aspects of comics and graphic novels, including their history, themes, and notable creators.

Document 1: Your and My Secret, known in Japan as lit.
the series was first published in Enix's Monthly Stencil
Document 2: Batman: Anarky is a 1999 trade paperback published by DC Comics.
The book collects prominent appearances of Anarky, a comic book character created by Alan Grant and Norm Breyfogle.
Document 3: It's a Good Life, If You Don't Weaken is a graphic novel by Canadian cartoonist Seth.
The mock-autobiographical story tells of its author's obsessive search for the work of a fictional forgotten cartoonist.
Document 4: Zenas Winsor McCay was an American cartoonist and animator.
He is best known for the comic strip Little Nemo and the animated film Gertie the Dinosaur.

topic.name: Mathematical Concepts and Theorems
topic.description: The documents discuss various mathematical concepts and theorems that are foundational in different fields of mathematics and applied sciences.

Document 1: Binary logarithm is the logarithm to the base 2.
Binary logarithms can be used to calculate the length of the representation of a number in the binary numeral system.
Document 2: Foster's reactance theorem is an important theorem in the fields of electrical network analysis and synthesis.
The proof of the theorem was presented by Ronald Martin Foster in 1924.
Document 3: A Hilbert space generalizes the notion of Euclidean space.
Hilbert spaces arise naturally and frequently in mathematics and physics.
Document 4: The roots of the reconstructed Proto-Indo-European language are basic parts of words that carry a lexical meaning.
Complete inflected words like verbs, nouns or adjectives are formed by adding further morphemes to a root.
Document 5: The derivative of a function of a real variable measures the sensitivity to change of a quantity.
Derivatives are a fundamental tool of calculus.
Document 6: The Dirac delta function is a generalized function on the real number line that is zero everywhere except at zero.
The delta function represents the density of an idealized point mass or point charge.
Document 7: A Taylor series is a representation of a function as an infinite sum of terms calculated from the values of the function's derivatives.
Taylor's theorem gives quantitative estimates on the error introduced by the use of such an approximation.

topic.name: Christian Music and Worship
topic.description: This topic encompasses various forms of music that express Christian themes, including genres like Christian metal and classical church compositions by composers such as Bach.

Document 1: Christian metal is a form of heavy metal music usually defined by its message using song lyrics as well as the dedication of the band members to Christianity.
Christian metal emerged in the late 1970s as a means of evangelization to the wider heavy metal music scene.
Document 2: Lobet Gott in seinen Reichen, BWV 11, is an oratorio by Johann Sebastian Bach, marked by him as Oratorium In Festo Ascensionis Xsti.
It follows the story of the Ascension as told in Luke, Mark and the Acts of the Apostles.
Document 3: Wahrlich, wahrlich, ich sage euch, BWV 86, is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
He composed it in Leipzig for Rogate, the fifth Sunday after Easter.
Document 4: Erforsche mich, Gott, und erfahre mein Herz, BWV 136, is a church cantata by Johann Sebastian Bach.
The opening movement is based on a verse from Psalm 139.

topic.name: Legal History and Activism
topic.description: This topic encompasses the evolution of legal systems, key legal figures, and significant activism related to law and rights.

Document 1: The Court of Common Pleas was a common law court in the English legal system.
The court's jurisdiction was gradually undercut by the King 's Bench and Exchequer of Pleas.
Document 2: Joseph Franklin Rutherford played a primary role in the organization and doctrinal development of Jehovah 's Witnesses.
He began a career in law, working as a court stenographer, trial lawyer and prosecutor.
Document 6: Mary Jane Rathbun, popularly known as Brownie Mary, was an American medical cannabis activist.
She lobbied for the legalization of cannabis for medical use.

topic.name: Religious Institutions and Communities
topic.description: This cluster discusses various religious congregations, their histories, and their roles in community development.

Document 1: Temple Israel is a Reform congregation located at 130 Riverside Drive in Dayton, Ohio.
The congregation moved to its current building in 1994.
Document 2: The Lutheran liturgical calendar is a listing which details the primary annual festivals and events that are celebrated liturgically by various Lutheran churches.
The purpose of the liturgical calendar is to guide commemorations as a part of the daily worship of the Lutheran Church.
Document 4: La Luz del Mundo is a Christian denomination with international headquarters in Guadalajara, Jalisco, Mexico.
The Church had its beginnings in 1926 just as Mexico plunged into a violent struggle between the anti-clerical government and Catholic rebels.
Document 5: St Tyfrydog's Church is a small medieval church in Llandyfrydog, Anglesey, north Wales.
The church is still in use for worship, as part of the Church in Wales.

topic.name: Notable Figures in Sports and Business
topic.description: This cluster discusses prominent individuals in sports and business, highlighting their achievements and controversies.

Document 1: Asashōryū was the 68th yokozuna in the history of the sport in Japan and became the first Mongol to reach sumo's highest rank.
He was criticized at times by the media and the Japan Sumo Association for not upholding the standards of behaviour expected of a holder of such a prestigious rank.
Document 2: Jerry Reinsdorf is an owner of the NBA's Chicago Bulls and the MLB's Chicago White Sox.
He is controversial for his involvement in breaking up the championship team by not hiring back key personnel such as Phil Jackson and Michael Jordan.
